slides.title: Your breath is a *dial for calm*.
slides.sub: Slow breathing speaks directly to your nervous system. Over time it may train real stress resilience.

slides.title: Breath talks to your body
slides.body: Your breathing is wired to your nervous system. Changing how you breathe *can shift* how calm you feel.

slides.title: Slow exhales settle you
slides.body: A longer out breath gently signals safety to the body. Heart rate eases and tension *may soften*.

slides.title: Try a simple rhythm
slides.body: Breathe in for four counts, out for six. Slow and unforced, letting the exhale lead.

slides.title: A few minutes a day
slides.body: Even five quiet minutes can help. Practiced often, slow breathing *is linked to* better stress resilience and HRV.

slides.title: A skill you carry
slides.body: Your breath is always with you, free and ready. Trained gently, it becomes a tool for hard moments.

slides.title: Train calm you can *carry*.
slides.body: Vibe Health helps you track the sleep, recovery and daily habits that build resilience, all in one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Your breath is a dial for calm that you carry everywhere. 🌿

Slow exhales signal safety to your nervous system, and a few quiet minutes a day are linked to better stress resilience and HRV. Try in for four, out for six. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one calm, simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Slow Breathing Trains Your Stress Resilience
